title: `atttypid` in `pg_attribute` table contains ID that does not exist on `pg_type` table
description: ### What happens? Creating a column that is `text` or `varchar` always has 25 in its `atttypid`, but that's not the one on `pg_type` records. ### To Reproduce 1. Create a table ```sql create table testtable ( textColumn text, varcharColumn varchar ); ``` 2. See `atttypid` in the table ```sql select c.relname, a.attname, a.atttypid from pg_class c join pg_namespace n on n.oid = c.relnamespace join pg_attribute a on a.attrelid = c.oid; ``` 3. Look up `oid`s in `pg_class` ```sql select oid, typname from pg_type where oid < 30 ``` No record found that has 25 in its `oid`. ### OS: MacOS ### DuckDB Version: 1.3.2 ### DuckDB Client: DuckDB UI ### Hardware: _No response_ ### Full Name: Seiya Izumi ### Affiliation: Tailor ### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build. I have tested with a stable release ### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue? Yes ### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have ### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE testtable (id Int32, textColumn String, varcharColumn String, nullableColumn Nullable(String));
INSERT INTO testtable (id, textColumn, varcharColumn, nullableColumn) VALUES (1, 'Sample text', 'Sample varchar', NULL);
SELECT 'testtable' AS relname, 'textColumn' AS attname, 0 AS atttypid WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM testtable WHERE textColumn IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id Int32, description String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY ref_id;
INSERT INTO another_table (ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 'Reference to testtable');
key_bug_features: Creating text or varchar columns with incorrect atttypid
